content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


June 19, 2002


Rhonda M. Fosbinder
Fosbinder & Fosbinder
415 Dairy Road, Suite E, #336
Kahului, HI 96732

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-146

Dear Ms. Fosbinder:

In response to your letter dated May 29, 2002, in which you seek advice concerning the status of the Entertainment Industry Development Corporation ("EIDC") as a "local government agency" under the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),¹ we are unable to provide you with the assistance you seek.

The Commission's Legal Division provides written advice to persons with questions about their duties under the Act. (Section 83114(b).) Under regulation 18329, copy enclosed, the Fair Political Practices Commission is authorized to provide advice only to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Regulations 18329(b)(1), 18329(c)(1).) The Commission gives advice so that persons can ascertain their duties under the Political Reform Act.

On the basis of the facts presented, it appears that the EIDC, or its authorized representative, is the appropriate party to seek advice on the question of if it qualifies as a "local government agency" under the Act. On these grounds, the Commission cannot specifically comment on the status of EIDC about which you inquire. I am sorry that at this time we cannot provide you with the guidance you requested.


¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-02-146
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West, Staff Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosure
